commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds live transport scenario capability metadata, a capability/driver/request-based planner, QA runtime re-exports, and focused QA runtime tests.

PR surface: Source +102, Tests +81. Total +183 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a PR adding QA planning API surface rather than a reported runtime bug.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is one narrow mechanical SDK contract alignment task that an automated repair lane could attempt if maintainers opt into it.

target.title: Add live transport scenario planning
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds required capability metadata to the existing standard live transport scenario catalog.
- Adds a planner that selects/skips standard live transport scenarios from available channel capabilities plus optional driver support/request filters.
- Re-exports the planning surface through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/qa-runtime` and covers it with QA runtime tests.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/qa-runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts`
- `pnpm lint:plugins:plugin-sdk-subpaths-exported`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Shared live transport QA scenario planning now has an explicit capability-to-standard-scenario mapping.
Real environment tested: Local source checkout in `/tmp/openclaw-user-flows`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/qa-runtime.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts`; `pnpm lint:plugins:plugin-sdk-subpaths-exported`; `git diff --check`.
Evidence after fix: The QA runtime tests pass with new selected/skipped planning assertions, the SDK alias test passes, and the SDK subpath export guard passes.
Observed result after fix: Standard scenario plans report selected cases and explicit skip reasons for missing capabilities, missing driver support, and request filtering.
What was not tested: No live Telegram/Slack/etc. lane was run; this PR adds the shared mapping/planning layer, not a channel-specific driver.
target.author: romneyda
